paragraphs.en: Homework has never been terribly popular with students and even many parents, but in recently years it has been particularly scorned. School districts across the country, most recently Los Angeles Unified, are revising their thinking on this educational ritual. Unfortunately, L.A. Unified has produced an inflexible policy which mandates that with the exception of some advanced courses, homework may no longer count for more than 10% of a student's academic grade.
paragraphs.zh: 家庭作业从未受到学生甚至许多家长的欢迎，但近年来它尤其受到蔑视。全国各地的学区，最近是洛杉矶联合大学，正在改变他们对这种教育仪式的看法。不幸的是，洛杉矶统一大学制定了一项不灵活的政策，要求除了一些高级课程外，家庭作业不得再占学生学术成绩的10 ％以上。

paragraphs.en: This rule is meant to address the difficulty that students from impoverished or chaotic homes might have in completing their homework. But the policy is unclear and contradictory. Certainly, no homework should be assigned that students cannot complete on their own or that they cannot do without expensive equipment. But if the district is essentially giving a pass to students who do not do their homework because of complicated family lives, it is going riskily close to the implication that standards need to be lowered for poor children.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: District administrators say that homework will still be a part of schooling; teachers are allowed to assign as much of it as they want. But with homework counting for no more than 10% of their grades, students can easily skip half their homework and see very little difference on their report cards. Some students might do well on state tests without completing their homework, but what about the students who performed well on the tests and did their homework? It is quite possible that the homework helped. Yet rather than empowering teachers to find what works best for their students, the policy imposes a flat, across­-the-­board rule.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: At the same time, the policy addresses none of the truly thorny questions about homework. If the district finds homework to be unimportant to its students' academic achievement, it should move to reduce or eliminate the assignments, not make them count for almost nothing. Conversely, if homework matters, it should account for a significant portion of the grade. Meanwhile, this policy does nothing to ensure that the homework students receive is meaningful or appropriate to their age and the subject, or that teachers are not assigning more than they are willing to review and correct.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: The homework rules should be put on hold while the school board, which is responsible for setting educational policy, looks into the matter and conducts public hearings. It is not too late for L. A. Unified to do homework right
paragraphs.zh: 在负责制定教育政策的学校董事会调查此事并举行公开听证会期间，应暂停家庭作业规则。L.A.Unified做好家庭作业还为时不晚

questions.text: It is implied in Paragraph 1 that nowadays homework________.
options: is no longer an educational ritual
is not required for advanced courses

questions.text: L.A. Unified has made the rule about homework mainly because poor students________.
options: tend to have moderate expectations for their education
have asked for a different educational standard
may have problems finishing their homework
have voiced their complaints about homework

questions.text: According to Paragraph 3, one problem with the policy is that it may________.
options: result in students' indifference to their report cards
undermine the authority of state tests

questions.text: As mentioned in Paragraph 4, a key question unanswered about homework is whether ______.
options: it places extra burdens on teachers

questions.text: A suitable title for this text could be________.
options: Wrong Interpretations of an Educational Policy
A Welcomed Policy for Poor Students

paragraphs.en: Pretty in pink: adult women do not remember being so obsessed with the colour, yet it is pervasive in our young girls' lives. It is not that pink is intrinsically bad, but it is such a tiny slice of the rainbow and, though it may celebrate girlhood in one way, it also repeatedly and firmly fuses girls' identity to appearance. Then it presents that connection, even among two­-year-­olds, between girls as not only innocent but as evidence of innocence. Looking around, I despaired at the singular lack of imagination about girls' lives and interests.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: Girls' attraction to pink may seem unavoidable, somehow encoded in their DNA, but according to Jo Paoletti, an associate professor of American Studies, it is not. Children were not colour­-coded at all until the early 20th century: in the era before domestic washing machines all babies wore white as a practical matter, since the only way of getting clothes clean was to boil them. What's more, both boys and girls wore what were thought of as gender-­neutral dresses. When nursery colours were introduced, pink was actually considered the more masculine colour, a pastel version of red, which was associated with strength. Blue, with its intimations of the Virgin Mary, constancy and faithfulness, symbolized femininity. It was not until the mid-­1980s，when amplifying age and sex differences became a dominant children's marketing strategy, that pink fully came into its own, when it began to seem inherently attractive to girls, part of what defined them as female, at least for the first few critical years.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: I had not realized how profoundly marketing trends dictated our perception of what is natural to kids, including our core beliefs about their psychological development. Take the toddler. I assumed that phase was something experts developed after years of research into children's behavior: wrong. Turns out, according to Daniel Cook, a historian of childhood consumerism, it was popularized as a marketing trick by clothing manufacturers in the 1930s.
paragraphs.zh: 我还没有意识到，营销趋势如何深刻地决定了我们对孩子自然的看法，包括我们对他们心理发展的核心信念。拿蹒跚学步的孩子来说。我认为这个阶段是专家在对儿童行为进行多年研究后开发的：错误。事实证明，根据童年消费主义历史学家丹尼尔·库克（ Daniel Cook ）的说法，它在20世纪30年代被服装制造商推广为一种营销技巧。

paragraphs.en: Trade publications counselled department stores that, in order to increase sales, they should create a “third stepping stone” between infant wear and older kids' clothes. It was only after “toddler” became a common shoppers' term that it evolved into a broadly accepted developmental stage. Splitting kids, or adults, into ever­tinier categories has proved a sure-­fire way to boost profits. And one of the easiest ways to segment a market is to magnify gender differences—or invent them where they did not previously exist.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

questions.text: By saying “it is…the rainbow” (Line 2, Para.1), the author means pink________.
options: should not be the sole representation of girlhood
should not be associated with girls' innocence
cannot explain girls' lack of imagination
cannot influence girls' lives and interests

questions.text: According to Paragraph 2, which of the following is true of colours?
options: Colors are encoded in girls' DNA.
Blue used to be regarded as the colour for girls.
Pink used to be a neutral colour in symbolizing genders.

questions.text: The author suggests that our perception of children's psychological development was much influenced by________.
options: the marketing of products for children

questions.text: We may learn from Paragraph 4 that department stores were advised to________.
options: focus on infant wear and older kids' clothes
attach equal importance to different genders

questions.text: It can be concluded that girls' attraction to pink seems to be________.
options: clearly explained by their inborn tendency

paragraphs.en: In 2010, a federal judge shook America's biotech industry to its core. Companies had won patents for isolated DNA for decades—by 2005 some 20% of human genes were patented. But in March 2012 a judge ruled that genes were unpatentable. Executives were violently agitated. The Biotechnology Industry Organization (BIO), a trade group, assured members that this was just a “preliminary step” in a longer battle.
paragraphs.zh: 2010年，一位联邦法官震撼了美国的生物技术产业。几十年来，公司已经获得了分离DNA的专利--到2005年，大约20%的人类基因获得了专利。但在2012年3月，一名法官裁定基因不可申请专利。高管们被激怒了。贸易组织生物技术产业组织（ BIO ）向成员们保证，这只是长期战斗中的“初步步骤”。

paragraphs.en: On July 29th they were relieved, at least temporarily. A federal appeals court overturned the prior decision, ruling that Myriad Genetics could indeed hold patents to two genes that help forecast a woman's risk of breast cancer. The chief executive of Myriad, a company in Utah, said the ruling was a blessing to firms and patients alike.
paragraphs.zh: 7月29日，他们得到了缓解，至少是暂时的。联邦上诉法院推翻了先前的裁决，裁定Myriad Genetics确实可以持有两种基因的专利，这两种基因有助于预测女性患乳腺癌的风险。犹他州公司Myriad的首席执行官表示，这项裁决对公司和患者都是一种祝福。

paragraphs.en: But as companies continue their attempts at personalized medicine, the courts will remain rather busy. The Myriad case itself is probably not over. Critics make three main arguments against gene patents: a gene is a product of nature, so it may not be patented; gene patents suppress innovation rather than reward it; and patents’ monopolies restrict access to genetic tests such as Myriad's. A growing number seem to agree. Last year a federal task­force urged reform for patents related to genetic tests. In October the Department of Justice filed a brief in the Myriad case, arguing that an isolated DNA molecule “is no less a product of nature…than are cotton fibres that have been separated from cotton seeds.”
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: Despite the appeals court's decision, big questions remain unanswered. For example, it is unclear whether the sequencing of a whole genome violates the patents of individual genes within it. The case may yet reach the Supreme Court.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管上诉法院做出了裁决，但重大问题仍未得到解答。例如，目前尚不清楚整个基因组的测序是否侵犯了其中单个基因的专利。此案仍可提交最高法院。

paragraphs.en: As the industry advances, however, other suits may have an even greater impact. Companies are unlikely to file many more patents for human DNA molecules—most are unlikely patented or in the public domain. Firms are now studying how genes interact, looking for correlations that might be used to determine the causes of disease or predict a drug's efficacy. Companies are eager to win patents for “connecting the dots，” explains Hans Sauer, a lawyers for the BIO.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，随着行业的发展，其他西装可能会产生更大的影响。公司不太可能为人类DNA分子申请更多专利-大多数不太可能获得专利或进入公共领域。公司现在正在研究基因如何相互作用，寻找可用于确定疾病原因或预测药物功效的相关性。BIO的律师汉斯·绍尔（ Hans Sauer ）解释说，公司渴望赢得“连接点”的专利。

paragraphs.en: Their success may be determined by a suit related to this issue, brought by the Mayo Clinic, which the Supreme Court will hear in its next term. The BIO recently held a convention which included sessions to coach lawyers on the shifting landscape for patents. Each meeting was packed.
paragraphs.zh: 他们的成功可能取决于梅奥诊所就此问题提起的诉讼，最高法院将在下届任期内审理该诉讼。BIO最近举行了一次会议，其中包括指导律师如何应对不断变化的专利局面。每次会议都挤满了人。

questions.text: It can be learned from Paragraph 1 that the biotech companies would like________.
options: judges to rule out gene patenting
the BIO to issue a warning

questions.text: Those who are against gene patents believe that________.
options: patents on genes depend much on innovation
courts should restrict access to genetic tests

questions.text: According to Hans Sauer, companies are eager to win patents for________.
By saying “Each meeting was packed”(Line 4，Para.6), the author means that________.
options: the BIO was a powerful organization
gene patenting was a great concern
lawyers were keen to attend conventions

questions.text: Generally speaking, the author's attitude toward gene patenting is________.

paragraphs.en: The great recession may be over, but this era of high joblessness is probably beginning. Before it ends, it will likely change the life course and character of a generation of young adults. And ultimately, it is likely to reshape our politics, our culture, and the character of our society for years.
paragraphs.zh: 大衰退可能已经结束，但这个高失业率的时代可能已经开始。在结束之前，它可能会改变一代年轻人的生活轨迹和性格。最终，它可能会重塑我们的政治、文化和社会性格多年。

paragraphs.en: No one tries harder than the jobless to find silver linings in this national economic disaster. Many said that unemployment, while extremely painful, had improved them in some ways: they had become less materialistic and more financially prudent; they were more aware of the struggles of others. In limited respects, perhaps the recession will leave society better off. At the very least, it has awoken us from our national fever dream of easy riches and bigger houses, and put a necessary end to an era of reckless personal spending.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: But for the most part, these benefits seem thin, uncertain, and far off. In The Moral Consequences of Economic Growth, the economic historian Benjamin Friedman argues that both inside and outside the U．S., lengthy periods of economic stagnation or decline have almost always left society more mean-­spirited and less inclusive, and have usually stopped or reversed the advance of rights and freedoms.Anti-­immigrant sentiment typically increases, as does conflict between races and classes.
paragraphs.zh: 但在大多数情况下，这些好处似乎很渺茫，不确定，而且遥不可及。在《经济增长的道德后果》（ The Moral Consequences of Economic Growth ）一书中，经济史学家本杰明·弗里德曼（ Benjamin Friedman ）认为，在美国内外，长期的经济停滞或衰退几乎总是让社会变得更加卑鄙，包容性更差，通常会阻止或逆转权利和自由的进步。反移民情绪通常会增加，种族和阶级之间的冲突也会增加。

paragraphs.en: Income inequality usually falls during a recession, but it has not shrunk in this one. Indeed, this period of economic weakness may reinforce class divides, and decrease opportunities to cross them—especially for young people. The research of Till Von Wachter, the economic at Columbia University, suggests that not all people graduating into a recession see their life chances dimmed: those with degrees from elite universities catch up fairly quickly to where they otherwise would have been if they had graduated in better times; it is the masses beneath them that are left behind.
paragraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

paragraphs.en: In the Internet age, it is particularly easy to see the resentment that has always been hidden within American society. More difficult, in the moment, is discerning precisely how these lean times are affecting society's character. In many respects, the U.S. was more socially tolerant entering this recession than at any time in its history, and a variety of national polls on social conflict since then have shown mixed results. We will have to wait and see exactly how these hard times will reshape our social fabric. But they certainly will reshape it, and all the more so the longer they extend.
